name: IApprovalGate
description: Contract for human-on-the-loop (HOTL) approval gate implementations. Manages approval requests for high-risk workflow actions, supporting both synchronous (blocking) and asynchronous (polling) approval patterns. Risk levels determine whether approval is required.

methods.name: request_approval

params.name: action
params.description: Description of the action requiring approval

params.name: risk_level
params.description: Risk level of the action (low, medium, high, critical)

methods.description: Request approval for an action and wait for a decision. Blocks until the approval is granted, denied, or times out. Returns an ApprovalResult with the decision and metadata.

methods.name: check_status

params.name: request_id
params.description: Unique identifier of a previously submitted approval request

methods.description: Check the current status of a previously submitted approval request. Returns the approval status (pending, approved, denied, timeout, escalated).

methods.name: request_approval_async

params.name: action
params.description: Description of the action requiring approval

params.name: risk_level
params.description: Risk level of the action (low, medium, high, critical)

methods.description: Submit an approval request without waiting for a decision. Returns a request_id that can be used with check_status() for polling. Useful for non-blocking approval workflows.

behavioral_expectations: Must implement structural subtyping (Protocol) — no explicit inheritance required
RiskLevel is an enum with values: low, medium, high, critical
ApprovalResult contains: request_id (str), status (ApprovalStatus), approver (str | None), timestamp (float), metadata (dict)
ApprovalStatus is an enum with values: pending, approved, denied, timeout, escalated
request_approval() must block until a decision is made or timeout occurs
request_approval_async() must return immediately with a request_id for polling
Low-risk actions may be auto-approved based on policy configuration
Critical-risk actions must always require explicit human approval
Timeout behavior is implementation-defined but must return a clear timeout result
